summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Mobile UI optimization. All mobile UI features working correctly - mobile header, bottom navigation, sidebar drawer, and responsive layouts.

issues: Bottom nav has extra padding for Emergent badge - this is expected behavior

Mobile header contains hamburger menu button
Mobile header contains notification bell
Mobile header contains create post button (+)
Mobile header contains HOMESTEAD branding
Mobile bottom navigation visible on mobile viewport

Sidebar drawer contains New Barter Post button
Sidebar drawer contains user info and Sign Out
Sidebar drawer closes on X button click

Navigation to Profile view works from bottom nav
Navigation to Feed view works from bottom nav

Desktop sidebar hidden on mobile (display: none)

Desktop layout works - sidebar visible (display: flex, width: 240px)

Desktop layout works - right panel visible (display: flex, width: 320px)
Desktop three-column layout preserved (sidebar left, feed center, right panel)
Feed displays cleanly on mobile without sidebar overlap
Profile page displays cleanly on mobile

critical_code_review_comments: WebSocket connection still failing on page load (pre-existing issue from iteration 1) - not related to mobile UI feature
Mobile UI CSS is well-structured with mobile-first approach
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile UI optimization fully tested and working. New components MobileHeader.js and MobileNav.js are functioning correctly. CSS media queries properly hide/show components based on viewport width. Mobile breakpoint is 768px. Desktop sidebar uses .sidebar class with display:flex on desktop. Mobile sidebar drawer uses .sidebar-mobile class with transform animation. Bottom navigation uses .mobile-nav class. All navigation flows work correctly on both mobile and desktop viewports.
notes: Mobile UI optimization is complete and working as expected. The mobile-first CSS approach ensures proper responsive behavior. All new components (MobileHeader, MobileNav) integrate seamlessly with the existing Dashboard component. The sidebar drawer animation is smooth and the overlay properly closes the drawer.
